text: DAY 69 ASSESSMENT: Pezeshkian confirms 2.5-hour meeting with Mojtaba Khamenei — first proof of life since March appointment. Significant because Supreme Leader's approval likely needed for any deal. Trump: 'very possible we'll make a deal.' Qatar PM echoes optimism. But Saeed Jalili (hardliner) reportedly replacing Ghalibaf as lead nuclear negotiator — complicates talks.
verificationNote: Al Arabiya and Euronews confirmed Pezeshkian-Khamenei meeting. Fox News reported Jalili replacement.

text: NUCLEAR INTEL: US intelligence assessment confirms Iran's nuclear breakout timeline UNCHANGED at 9-12 months despite months of US/Israeli strikes on nuclear sites. All enriched material still in Iran's possession, likely in deeply buried underground facilities. Contradicts Trump admin claims of having 'obliterated' Iran's nuclear capabilities.
verificationNote: US News exclusive confirmed by Haaretz. Multiple intelligence sources cited.

text: MARKETS: S&P 500 surges to 7,365 (+1.46%) on US-Iran deal optimism. Oil recovering: Brent $101.96 (+0.68%), WTI $93.46 (-1.7% intraday range). Gas still $4.558 — pump prices lag oil by days to weeks. If deal goes through and Hormuz reopens, analysts see Brent dropping to $70-80 range within weeks.
verificationNote: CNBC confirmed market movements and oil prices. Trading Economics verified.

text: ISRAELI POLL: Haaretz survey — 59% of Israelis say ending war NOW is not in Israel's security interest. 62% say likelihood of return to widescale conflict is high. Israeli security establishment 'unanimously opposes' US deal framework, calling it 'disaster for Israel.' Netanyahu reportedly preparing contingency to act unilaterally if US-Iran deal proceeds.
verificationNote: Haaretz published poll data. Multiple Israeli outlets confirmed security establishment opposition.

text: IRGC KURDS: IRGC launched drone strikes on Kurdish Iranian opposition groups in northern Iraq — Komala camp near Sulaymaniyah (May 5) and KDPI bases at Zewi Aspi near Koya and Girde Chal near Erbil (May 6). Tehran opening secondary front against Kurdish dissidents while main war continues.
verificationNote: FDD Long War Journal and Rudaw confirmed IRGC drone strikes on Kurdish opposition in Iraq.

text: DIPLOMACY TRACKER: Multiple channels active — (1) Pakistan mediating US-Iran MOU response due today, (2) Araghchi endorsed China's 4-point plan in Beijing, (3) Trump-Xi summit May 14-15, (4) UN Security Council closed consultations ongoing. Qatar PM: 'high probability' of deal. Key risk: Jalili replacing Ghalibaf could shift Iran's negotiating posture hawkish.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera compiled all diplomatic channels. Times of Israel confirmed Qatar PM statement.

text: LEBANON CEASEFIRE COLLAPSE: Israeli airstrikes hammer southern Lebanon May 7. At least 4 killed in Zelaya (incl. 2 women, elderly man). Three strikes on Dibbin (Marjayoun district), three IAF strikes on Nabatieh. Lebanon Health Ministry confirms casualties. New evacuation orders for Deir al-Zahrani, Bafroa, Habush. April 17 truce essentially ended after May 6 Beirut strike on Hezbollah Radwan commander Ballout.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera and Lebanon Health Ministry confirmed casualty figures. The National confirmed expanded displacement orders.

text: ISW UPDATE: IRGC Commander Ahmad Vahidi has prevailed in Iranian regime's internal power struggle, marginalizing 'pragmatist' actors and dictating Tehran's hardline approach toward Strait of Hormuz. Iran is attempting to demonstrate 'control' via attacks on UAE/Oman commercial vessels. Pezeshkian's call for lifting US naval blockade FIRST aligns with Vahidi consolidation.
verificationNote: Critical Threats Project Iran Updates May 4 and May 5 confirmed Vahidi consolidation of power.

text: FRENCH CARRIER ARRIVES: FS Charles de Gaulle (R91) and escort group transited Suez Canal May 6, en route to southern Red Sea/Gulf of Aden ahead of potential multinational Hormuz freedom-of-navigation operation. Air wing: 20 Rafales, 2 E-2C Hawkeyes, 3 helicopters. France/UK leading 40-country coalition. Project Freedom paused pending Iran response to MOU.
verificationNote: Defense News and Naval News confirmed Charles de Gaulle Suez transit. The National confirmed coalition size.

text: POPE-TRUMP CLASH ESCALATES: Pope Leo XIV at Castel Gandolfo: 'Mission of the Church is to proclaim the Gospel, to preach peace.' Reaffirmed Catholic Church does NOT support nuclear weapons. Trump fired back, accusing Pope of 'endangering a lot of Catholics.' PolitiFact rated Trump's earlier claim that Pope supports Iran nukes as FALSE (April 13). Rubio scheduled Vatican meeting for May 7.
verificationNote: Time and CNN confirmed Pope Leo statements. PolitiFact published fact-check April 13.

text: TREASURY SANCTIONS: US Treasury announced May 1 sanctions targeting 6 individuals, 21 entities, 1 vessel — 12 China-based, 3 Iran-based, 5 UAE-based, 1 UK-based. Targets include Qingdao Haiye Oil Terminal (China) and 3 Iranian foreign currency exchange houses. ~40 shipping firms in Iran 'shadow fleet' sanctioned to date. 12th round of Iran oil action since NSPM-2 (Feb 2025).
verificationNote: US Treasury press release SB0472 and State Department briefing confirmed sanctions.

text: GAS PRICE PEAK: AAA national average climbs to $4.558/gal (+49.4% from $3.05 pre-war) — 4-year high. State records: California >$8/gal record; Michigan >$6; Illinois & Wisconsin record highs. Diesel ~$5.65/gal nationally, within ~20¢ of all-time record. Brent $101.96 partial recovery from May 6 crash. If MOU finalized, gas could ease 20-30¢ within 1-2 weeks.
verificationNote: AAA daily fuel prices and AgWeb diesel reporting confirmed state records.

text: HORMUZ CLASH: IRGC attacked 3 US Navy destroyers (Truxtun, Rafael Peralta, Mason) with missiles, drones, and fast-attack boats in the Strait of Hormuz. All intercepted. CENTCOM struck back at Qeshm Island, Bandar Abbas, Bandar Khamir, Sirik. 6 IRGC boats destroyed. No US casualties. Iran claims retaliatory strikes near Chabahar hit US ships (CENTCOM denies). Trump: 'just a love tap.'
verificationNote: CNN, NPR, Al Jazeera, CBS News all confirmed CENTCOM operations and Iranian attack details.

text: IRAN PGSA: Iran created 'Persian Gulf Strait Authority' — new government agency requiring 40+ question 'Vessel Information Declaration' for all ships. Lloyd's: PGSA 'positioning itself as only valid authority.' Maritime law experts say this violates UNCLOS. Only 40 ships crossed strait in past week vs. pre-war 120/day. Iran attempting to create permanent gatekeeper role regardless of deal outcome.
verificationNote: CNN, Maritime Executive, Fortune, PBS confirmed PGSA creation and details.

text: TRUMP ULTIMATUM: 'If they don't agree, the bombing starts, and it will be at a much higher level and intensity.' Gives Iran one week. Called Hormuz strikes 'just a love tap.' Says ceasefire 'still in effect' despite exchange of fire. Aiming for deal before May 14-15 Beijing summit with Xi. Rep. Barrett (R-MI) introduces 90-day AUMF — prohibits ground troops.
verificationNote: Fox News, CNN, CNBC confirmed Trump quotes. Barrett AUMF via press release.

text: HEZBOLLAH RETALIATION: Record 105 attack waves on northern Israel — most intense day since war began. Nahariya: 1 killed, 14 wounded from rocket strikes. 10 rockets at Karmiel, 30+ at other locations. Clear retaliation for IDF killing of Radwan Force commander Ballout in Beirut (first strike since April 16 ceasefire). IDF killed 2 Hezbollah + 3 PIJ in response.
verificationNote: Times of Israel, Media Line confirmed Nahariya casualties. Wikipedia timeline confirmed 105 attack waves.

text: UAE UNDER ATTACK AGAIN: UAE intercepted fresh Iranian missile and drone attacks May 8 following Hormuz clash. Dubai schools remain on distance learning (Day 4). Arrow-3 crisis: Knesset emergency meeting over Israel's interceptor shortage — Haaretz reports Iran making missiles faster than Israel producing interceptors. Israel partnering with US manufacturer for accelerated production.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera liveblog and Times of Israel confirmed UAE intercepts. Haaretz confirmed Arrow-3 production gap.

text: OIL REBOUND: Brent $101.26 (+26.9% from pre-war), WTI $95.64 (+26.7%) — rallying on Hormuz fire exchange. Both benchmarks still down ~6% for the week. OPEC+ approved 188K bpd increase for June (first meeting without UAE, which quit May 1). IEA: 411.9M barrels deployed from strategic reserves. Rubio met Pope Leo at Vatican — 45 min on Iran war. Pezeshkian-Macron call: accused US of 'stabbing from behind.'
verificationNote: CNBC confirmed oil prices and OPEC+ decision. CNN confirmed Rubio-Pope meeting. ANI confirmed Macron-Pezeshkian call.
